top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
62 miles on paved Veneto roads through the UNESCO Prosecco burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
5,118ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco)'s paved Veneto roads through the UNESCO Prosecco forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
83ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco). Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,118ft of climbing on paved Veneto roads through the UNESCO Prosecco does to your power file on race day.
At Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Veneto roads through the UNESCO Prosecco. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco). Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco), that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco)’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 62 miles of paved veneto roads through the unesco prosecco hills wine region in Valdobbiadene, Veneto, Italy.
